testcase.name: runScenario["Shadow DOM page loads successfully", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](1)
testcase.system-err: SLF4J: Failed to load class "org.slf4j.impl.StaticLoggerBinder". SLF4J: Defaulting to no-operation (NOP) logger implementation SLF4J: See http://www.slf4j.org/codes.html#StaticLoggerBinder for further details.

testcase.name: runScenario["Shadow host element is present and accessible", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](2)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @smoke @visibility Scenario: Shadow host element is present and accessible # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:20
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: Shadow host element is present and accessible [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() Then a shadow host element should exist on the page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.aShadowHostElementShouldExistOnThePage() And the shadow root should be accessible # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.theShadowRootShouldBeAccessible() [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: Shadow host element is present and accessible → PASSED

testcase.name: runScenario["Shadow DOM widgets render visible text content", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](3)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @smoke @content Scenario: Shadow DOM widgets render visible text content # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:25
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: Shadow DOM widgets render visible text content [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() [Assert] Shadow text (80 chars): 'SHADOW DOM FORM This entire widget lives inside a Shadow DOM. Use Playwright's p...' Then the shadow DOM should contain visible text content # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.theShadowDomShouldContainVisibleTextContent() And the shadow content should not be empty # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.theShadowContentShouldNotBeEmpty() [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: Shadow DOM widgets render visible text content → PASSED

testcase.name: runScenario["User can type into the shadow DOM form input", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](4)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @widget1 @form @input Scenario: User can type into the shadow DOM form input # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:34
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: User can type into the shadow DOM form input [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() [Step] Typed 'ShadowTest@vault.com' into shadow form input When I type "ShadowTest@vault.com" into the shadow form input # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iTypeIntoTheShadowFormInput(java.lang.String) [Assert] Form input value: 'ShadowTest@vault.com' Then the shadow form input should contain "ShadowTest@vault.com" # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.theShadowFormInputShouldContain(java.lang.String) [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: User can type into the shadow DOM form input → PASSED

testcase.name: runScenario["User can clear and retype in the shadow DOM form input", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](5)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @widget1 @form @input Scenario: User can clear and retype in the shadow DOM form input # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:39
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: User can clear and retype in the shadow DOM form input [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() [Step] Typed 'initial text' into shadow form input When I type "initial text" into the shadow form input # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iTypeIntoTheShadowFormInput(java.lang.String) [Step] Shadow form input cleared And I clear the shadow form input # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iClearTheShadowFormInput() [Step] Typed 'updated text' into shadow form input And I type "updated text" into the shadow form input # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iTypeIntoTheShadowFormInput(java.lang.String) [Assert] Form input value: 'updated text' Then the shadow form input should contain "updated text" # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.theShadowFormInputShouldContain(java.lang.String) [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: User can clear and retype in the shadow DOM form input → PASSED

testcase.name: runScenario["Submit button is enabled and clickable", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](6)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @widget1 @form @submit Scenario: Submit button is enabled and clickable # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:46
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: Submit button is enabled and clickable [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() Then the shadow submit button should be enabled # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.theShadowSubmitButtonShouldBeEnabled() [Step] Clicked SUBMIT button When I click the shadow DOM submit button # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iClickTheShadowDomSubmitButton() Then no JS errors should be present in the console # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.noJsErrorsShouldBePresentInTheConsole() [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: Submit button is enabled and clickable → PASSED

testcase.name: runScenario["User can fill the form and submit it", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](7)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @widget1 @form @submit Scenario: User can fill the form and submit it # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:52
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: User can fill the form and submit it [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() [Step] Typed 'test@example.com' into shadow form input When I type "test@example.com" into the shadow form input # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iTypeIntoTheShadowFormInput(java.lang.String) [Step] Clicked SUBMIT button And I click the shadow DOM submit button # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iClickTheShadowDomSubmitButton() Then no JS errors should be present in the console # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.noJsErrorsShouldBePresentInTheConsole() [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: User can fill the form and submit it → PASSED

testcase.name: runScenario["Shadow form input accepts various email formats", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](8)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @widget1 @form @parameterised Scenario Outline: Shadow form input accepts various email formats # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:64
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: Shadow form input accepts various email formats [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() [Step] Typed 'user@example.com' into shadow form input When I type "user@example.com" into the shadow form input # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iTypeIntoTheShadowFormInput(java.lang.String) [Assert] Form input value: 'user@example.com' Then the shadow form input should contain "user@example.com" # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.theShadowFormInputShouldContain(java.lang.String) [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: Shadow form input accepts various email formats → PASSED

testcase.name: runScenario["Shadow form input accepts various email formats", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](9)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @widget1 @form @parameterised Scenario Outline: Shadow form input accepts various email formats # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:65
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: Shadow form input accepts various email formats [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() [Step] Typed 'test.user+tag@domain.org' into shadow form input When I type "test.user+tag@domain.org" into the shadow form input # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iTypeIntoTheShadowFormInput(java.lang.String) [Assert] Form input value: 'test.user+tag@domain.org' Then the shadow form input should contain "test.user+tag@domain.org" # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.theShadowFormInputShouldContain(java.lang.String) [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: Shadow form input accepts various email formats → PASSED

testcase.name: runScenario["Shadow form input accepts various email formats", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](10)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @widget1 @form @parameterised Scenario Outline: Shadow form input accepts various email formats # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:66
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: Shadow form input accepts various email formats [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() [Step] Typed 'qa_engineer@vault.io' into shadow form input When I type "qa_engineer@vault.io" into the shadow form input # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iTypeIntoTheShadowFormInput(java.lang.String) [Assert] Form input value: 'qa_engineer@vault.io' Then the shadow form input should contain "qa_engineer@vault.io" # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.theShadowFormInputShouldContain(java.lang.String) [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: Shadow form input accepts various email formats → PASSED

testcase.name: runScenario["Counter starts at zero on page load", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](11)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @widget2 @counter Scenario: Counter starts at zero on page load # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:74
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: Counter starts at zero on page load [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() [Assert] Counter value: '0' (expected '0') Then the shadow counter value should be "0" # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.theShadowCounterValueShouldBe(java.lang.String) [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: Counter starts at zero on page load → PASSED

testcase.name: runScenario["Increment button increases the counter", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](12)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @widget2 @counter Scenario: Increment button increases the counter # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:78
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: Increment button increases the counter [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() [Step] Clicking + button 3 times [Counter] + clicked → 1 [Counter] + clicked → 2 [Counter] + clicked → 3 When I click the increment button 3 times # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iClickTheIncrementButtonTimes(int) [Assert] Counter value: '3' (expected '3') Then the shadow counter value should be "3" # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.theShadowCounterValueShouldBe(java.lang.String) [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: Increment button increases the counter → PASSED

testcase.name: runScenario["Clicking increment and decrement buttons does not throw JS errors", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](13)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @widget2 @counter Scenario: Clicking increment and decrement buttons does not throw JS errors # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:83
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: Clicking increment and decrement buttons does not throw JS errors [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() [Step] Clicking + button 2 times [Counter] + clicked → 1 [Counter] + clicked → 2 When I click the increment button 2 times # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iClickTheIncrementButtonTimes(int) [Step] Clicking - button 1 times [Counter] - clicked → 2 And I click the decrement button 1 times # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iClickTheDecrementButtonTimes(int) Then no JS errors should be present in the console # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.noJsErrorsShouldBePresentInTheConsole() [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: Clicking increment and decrement buttons does not throw JS errors → PASSED

testcase.name: runScenario["Shadow select dropdown has options available", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](14)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @widget3 @select Scenario: Shadow select dropdown has options available # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:93
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: Shadow select dropdown has options available [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() [Assert] Select option count: 5 Then the shadow select should have more than 1 option # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.theShadowSelectShouldHaveMoreThanOption(int) [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: Shadow select dropdown has options available → PASSED

testcase.name: runScenario["User can select the second option in the shadow dropdown", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](15)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @widget3 @select Scenario: User can select the second option in the shadow dropdown # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:97
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: User can select the second option in the shadow dropdown [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() [Select] Index 1 → 'Playwright' When I select option at index 1 from the shadow dropdown # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iSelectOptionAtIndexFromTheShadowDropdown(int) [Assert] Selected option: 'Playwright' Then the shadow select should not show the default placeholder # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.theShadowSelectShouldNotShowTheDefaultPlaceholder() [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: User can select the second option in the shadow dropdown → PASSED

testcase.name: runScenario["User can select multiple options in sequence without errors", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](16)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @widget3 @select Scenario: User can select multiple options in sequence without errors # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:102
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: User can select multiple options in sequence without errors [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() [Select] Index 1 → 'Playwright' When I select option at index 1 from the shadow dropdown # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iSelectOptionAtIndexFromTheShadowDropdown(int) [Select] Index 2 → 'Cypress' And I select option at index 2 from the shadow dropdown # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iSelectOptionAtIndexFromTheShadowDropdown(int) [Assert] Selected option: 'Cypress' Then the shadow select should not show the default placeholder # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.theShadowSelectShouldNotShowTheDefaultPlaceholder() And no JS errors should be present in the console # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.noJsErrorsShouldBePresentInTheConsole() [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: User can select multiple options in sequence without errors → PASSED

testcase.name: runScenario["Shadow DOM elements are reachable by keyboard Tab navigation", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](17)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @keyboard @accessibility Scenario: Shadow DOM elements are reachable by keyboard Tab navigation # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:113
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: Shadow DOM elements are reachable by keyboard Tab navigation [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() [Step] Page body focused When I focus the page body # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iFocusThePageBody() [Step] Tabbed 5 times And I press Tab to navigate into the shadow DOM # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iPressTabToNavigateIntoTheShadowDom() Then a shadow DOM element should receive focus # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.aShadowDomElementShouldReceiveFocus() [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: Shadow DOM elements are reachable by keyboard Tab navigation → PASSED

testcase.name: runScenario["Shadow DOM roots are accessible via JavaScript traversal", "VAULT Shadow DOM Interactions"](18)
testcase.system-out:
@ShadowDom @nested @advanced Scenario: Shadow DOM roots are accessible via JavaScript traversal # src/test/resources/features/shadow_dom.feature:123
[VAULT] ▶ Starting scenario: Shadow DOM roots are accessible via JavaScript traversal [Page] Navigated to: https://ml-minds.com/shadow-dom Given I navigate to the VAULT Shadow DOM page # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.iNavigateToTheVaultShadowDomPage() Then nested shadow DOM elements should be reachable via JavaScript piercing # com.vault.shadowdom.steps.ShadowDomSteps.nestedShadowDomElementsShouldBeReachableViaJavaScriptPiercing() [VAULT] ■ Finished scenario: Shadow DOM roots are accessible via JavaScript traversal → PASSED
